title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

gpu: Intel Open Source Technology Center Mesa DRI Intel(R) Haswell Server x86/MMX/SSE2
notes: Follow the advice here https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/298#issuecomment-423711864 but also tell protontricks to install directmusic and set sound=alsa. It will then work perfectly.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

gpu: Intel Open Source Technology Center Mesa DRI Intel(R) UHD Graphics 620 (Kabylake GT2)
notes: Quick test of the Campaign. After running the following "mv ${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Rise\ of\ Nations/patriots.exe{,.bak}" and "ln -s riseofnations.exe ${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Rise\ of\ Nations/patriots.exe" the game launches and runs at about 45fps at 1080p however there is no audio
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% for flickering, protontricks directmusic, protontricks sound=alsa. After that, it works perfectly and stable (several hours). Did not test multiplayer.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: The game does not start in any way.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: No sound, graphics and controls works perfectly
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% for flickering, protontricks directmusic, protontricks sound=alsa. After that, it works perfectly and stable
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: After wiping previous prefix, it ran immediately & stable without sound + movies. Sound worked after 'protontricks 287450 directsound' and 'protontricks 287450 sound=alsa'. Just for stability I added launch option 'PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%'. Multiplayer cannot connect to servers with current version, but it does connect with v.1.10 (Beta Options in Steam, and disabling vSync in rise2.ini removed strange mouse behavior). So you could at least play with others on that version.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: I've rated it as silver, but I think some of the issues present in proton are also present in the native Windows version.  For example, the forums have quite a few people saying they can't connect to multiplayer games unless they roll back to version 1.10. With the sound workaround everything is working great!  I can connect to the servers with version 1.10, but I have not tried a multiplayer game.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: in Main Menu, Display is blinking, but in game, it looks fine. Multiplayer was not possible (no connection to servers)
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

cpu: AMD A10-9620P RADEON R5, 10 COMPUTE CORES 4C+6G
notes: Sound worked after 'protontricks 287450 directmusic' and 'protontricks 287450 sound=alsa'. Just for stability I added launch option 'PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%'. tutorial and scenario crashes but quick match seem to work
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Works without any major issues after setting launch options to 'PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%' and some "winecraft" with protontricks the other people mentioned, namely: protontricks directmusic and protontricks sound=alsa (thanks!). As for the minor issues the starting cinematics do not work (black screen, only cursor is visible, they are skippable though) and some glitches in the UI (upper right corner's font has some artifacts).
